# @jswork/react-interactive-list
> A React interactive list component with full CRUD operations, reorder commands, event bus system, and slot-based rendering. Designed for building dynamic, editable list UIs with minimal boilerplate. Written in TypeScript with React 18+ support.

**Important notes for agents:**
- This is a single-component library focused on interactive list management (add/remove/reorder/update).
- The component uses an internal event bus (`@jswork/event-mitt`) to decouple commands from UI.
- The `useCommand` hook is the recommended way to control the list from parent or child components.
- Slot-based rendering (`slots.item` / `slots.empty`) replaces traditional `template` / `renderItem` props.

## Installation

```bash
npm install -S @jswork/react-interactive-list
```

Peer dependencies (install if not present):
```bash
npm install react react-dom @jswork/event-mitt @jswork/harmony-events @jswork/react-list classnames fast-deep-equal
```

## Imports

```tsx
// Published package
import ReactInteractiveList, { useCommand } from '@jswork/react-interactive-list/main';

// Or from source (during development)
import ReactInteractiveList, { useCommand } from '@jswork/react-interactive-list/src/main';
```

## Component: ReactInteractiveList

### Props (ReactInteractiveListProps)

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | `string` | `'@'` | Identity name for event bus scoping. Must match `useCommand(name)`. |
| `value` | `any[]` | `[]` | Controlled data source array. |
| `slots` | `{ item: Slot, empty?: Slot }` | — | **Required.** Slot components for rendering items and empty state. |
| `keyExtractor` | `string \| ((item, index) => string)` | — | Key field name or extractor function for list items. |
| `defaults` | `() => any` | — | **Required.** Factory function that returns a new item object for `add()`. |
| `initial` | `number` | `0` | Auto-fill items to this count on mount. |
| `min` | `number` | `0` | Minimum allowed items. Triggers `onError('EQ_MIN')` if breached. |
| `max` | `number` | `100` | Maximum allowed items. Triggers `onError('EQ_MAX')` if exceeded. |
| `onChange` | `(value, options?) => void` | — | Called with new array on every mutation. `options` includes `action`, `payload`, `oldValue`, `newValue`. |
| `onError` | `(code: 'EQ_MIN' \| 'EQ_MAX') => void` | — | Called when list size hits `min` or `max` boundary. |
| `options` | `any` | — | Extra context passed through to `onChange` callback options. |
| `className` | `string` | — | Additional CSS class name. |
| `forwardedRef` | `Ref` | — | Ref to the underlying div element. |

### Slot Types

```tsx
// Slot can be a React component or a render function
type Slot<T> = React.ComponentType<T> | ((props: T) => React.ReactNode);

// Item slot receives:
slots.item: Slot<{ item: any; index: number; data: any[] }>

// Empty slot receives:
slots.empty?: Slot<{ data: any[] }>
```

## Hook: useCommand

The `useCommand` hook returns command functions that communicate with the component via the event bus. Pass the same `name` as the component's `name` prop.

```tsx
const { add, remove, update, set, up, down, top, bottom, clear, notify, listen } = useCommand('i1');
```

### Commands

| Command | Signature | Description |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| `add()` | `() => void` | Append a new item (created via `defaults()`) to the end of the list. Respects `max` limit. |
| `remove(opts)` | `(index: number \| { index, action, payload? }) => void` | Remove item at index. Pass a number for simple removal, or an object for custom action tracking. Respects `min` limit. |
| `update(opts)` | `({ index, item, action, payload? }) => void` | Replace item at `index` with `item`. |
| `set(items)` | `(any[]) => void` | Replace the entire list with a new array. |
| `up(index)` | `(number) => void` | Move item at `index` one position up. No-op if already first. |
| `down(index)` | `(number) => void` | Move item at `index` one position down. No-op if already last. |
| `top(index)` | `(number) => void` | Move item at `index` to the first position. |
| `bottom(index)` | `(number) => void` | Move item at `index` to the last position. |
| `clear()` | `() => void` | Remove all items (sets to `[]`). |
| `notify(opts?)` | `({ action?, payload? }) => void` | Trigger `onChange` without changing data. Useful for signaling external edits (e.g., inline field changes). |
| `listen(cmd, callback)` | `(string, Function) => { destroy: () => void }` | Listen for a specific command event. Returns a subscription with `destroy()` for cleanup. |

### onChange Callback Options

Every mutation triggers `onChange` with these metadata:

```tsx
type OnChangeCallbackOptions = {
  name: string;          // Component's name prop
  options: any;          // Extra options prop
  action: string;        // 'add' | 'remove' | 'update' | 'set' | 'up' | 'down' | 'top' | 'bottom' | 'clear' | 'notify'
  payload?: any;         // Custom payload from remove/update/notify
  oldValue: any[];       // Previous list value
  newValue: any[];       // New list value
}
```

## Static Properties

```tsx
ReactInteractiveList.displayName  // 'react-interactive-list'
ReactInteractiveList.events       // ['add','remove','cancel','set','up','down','top','bottom','clear','notify','change']
ReactInteractiveList.event        // Global event bus instance (EventMitt)
```

## Usage Examples

### Basic Setup

```tsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import ReactInteractiveList, { useCommand } from '@jswork/react-interactive-list/main';

// Define your item component (receives item, index, data via slot props)
const ItemSlot = ({ item, index, data }) => (
  <div>
    <span>{index + 1}. {item.title}</span>
  </div>
);

// Define empty state component
const EmptySlot = () => <div>No items yet.</div>;

function App() {
  const [items, setItems] = useState([
    { id: '1', title: 'First item' },
    { id: '2', title: 'Second item' },
  ]);
  const { add, remove, clear } = useCommand('myList');

  return (
    <div>
      <button onClick={add}>Add</button>
      <button onClick={() => remove(0)}>Remove First</button>
      <button onClick={clear}>Clear All</button>

      <ReactInteractiveList
        name="myList"
        value={items}
        slots={{ item: ItemSlot, empty: EmptySlot }}
        keyExtractor="id"
        defaults={() => ({ id: String(Date.now()), title: 'New item' })}
        onChange={(value) => setItems(value)}
      />
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Reorder Controls Inside Item Slot

```tsx
const ItemSlot = ({ item, index, data }) => {
  const { up, down, top, bottom, remove } = useCommand('myList');

  return (
    <div>
      <button disabled={index === 0} onClick={() => up(index)}>Up</button>
      <button disabled={index === data.length - 1} onClick={() => down(index)}>Down</button>
      <button onClick={() => top(index)}>To Top</button>
      <button onClick={() => bottom(index)}>To Bottom</button>
      <button onClick={() => remove(index)}>Delete</button>
      <span>{item.title}</span>
    </div>
  );
};
```

### Inline Editing with notify

```tsx
const ItemSlot = ({ item, index }) => {
  const { notify } = useCommand('myList');

  return (
    <input
      value={item.title}
      onChange={(e) => {
        item.title = e.target.value;
        notify(); // triggers onChange without adding/removing items
      }}
    />
  );
};
```

### Event Listening with cleanup

```tsx
function App() {
  const { listen } = useCommand('myList');

  useEffect(() => {
    const sub = listen('remove', (args) => {
      console.log('Item removed:', args);
    });
    return () => sub.destroy(); // cleanup on unmount
  }, []);

  // ...
}
```

### Min/Max Boundaries

```tsx
<ReactInteractiveList
  name="myList"
  value={items}
  slots={slots}
  keyExtractor="id"
  defaults={defaults}
  min={1}     // at least 1 item required
  max={10}    // at most 10 items allowed
  onError={(code) => {
    if (code === 'EQ_MIN') console.warn('Minimum items reached');
    if (code === 'EQ_MAX') console.warn('Maximum items reached');
  }}
  onChange={handleChange}
/>
```

### Initial Items Auto-fill

```tsx
// If list is empty on mount, auto-fill to 3 items using defaults()
<ReactInteractiveList
  name="myList"
  initial={3}
  value={items}
  slots={slots}
  keyExtractor="id"
  defaults={() => ({ id: crypto.randomUUID(), title: '' })}
  onChange={handleChange}
/>
```

## Event Bus

The component uses `@jswork/event-mitt` as a global event bus. Events are scoped by `name`:

- Pattern: `{name}:{command}` (e.g., `myList:add`, `myList:remove`)
- Change event: `{name}:change` (emitted on every mutation with full callback options)

You can subscribe directly:

```tsx
ReactInteractiveList.event.on('myList:change', (opts) => {
  console.log(opts.action, opts.oldValue, opts.newValue);
});
```

## Dependencies

- `@jswork/react-list` — Base list rendering component with slot system
- `@jswork/event-mitt` — Lightweight event emitter
- `@jswork/harmony-events` — Auto-wires static events to component methods
- `classnames` — CSS class utility
- `fast-deep-equal` — Deep equality comparison for state sync

## Links

- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.org/package/@jswork/react-interactive-list)
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/afeiship/react-interactive-list)
- [Live demo](https://afeiship.github.io/react-interactive-list/)
- [License: MIT](https://github.com/afeiship/react-interactive-list/blob/master/LICENSE.txt)

## Usage Notes for Agents

- Always set `name` prop on the component and pass the same name to `useCommand(name)` — they must match.
- The `slots.item` component receives `{ item, index, data }` as props (not `template` or `renderItem`).
- Use `keyExtractor` with a string field name (e.g., `"id"`) for simple cases, or a function for complex keys.
- The `defaults` function must return a new object each time — it is called by `add()`.
- `onChange` is the single source of truth for state updates — always update your state here.
- `notify()` is the correct way to trigger re-render after mutating item fields in-place (e.g., editing input values).
- The component supports controlled mode: when `value` prop changes externally, the component syncs internal state.
- Use `listen()` with cleanup in `useEffect` to avoid memory leaks.
